Ad Text:

Contract to 30/06/2026 plus extensions| Fortitude Valley| Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland Government client to recruit experienced Senior Technical Analyst. In this role, you will identify, analyse, and document cyber security system and application requirements, within a large Information Management & ICT environment, to ensure the delivery of valued, integrated, and effective technical and business solutions to projects.

Provide a support role for other business analysts, technical analysts, technical consultants and change managers within the program/projects

What we’re looking for?

Proven experience performing technical analysis activities within cyber program/project(s), preferably in the health care industry

Demonstrated experience in monitoring, management, and automation tools for firewalls

Demonstrated knowledge and experience in technology domains, such as network and cloud security (firewalls, SaaS, secure web gateway)

Demonstrated knowledge and experience in release planning and piloting/deploying solutions to the end points in a large-scale setup

Proven experience in identify and documenting applications interfaces and systems interconnectivity that would allow integration partners to understand and leverage the use of existing interfaces

Job Title: Category Strategy Manager – Portfolio, Innovation & Growth
Ad Text:
Shape the future of the global wine category.  

Vinarchy is a global wine company with iconic brands including Jacob’s Creek, Hardys and Campo Viejo, operating at scale across multiple markets. As we continue to bring Vinarchy to life, there is significant opportunity to shape, align and elevate category strategy, portfolio direction and ways of working globally.

This is a high‑impact global role within our Global Marketing team, reporting to the  

Global Innovation Director.  

The Role  

As Category Strategy Manager – Portfolio, Innovation & Growth, you will lead category‑led growth across Vinarchy’s global portfolio. You’ll identify where the category should play, how innovation and portfolio strategy unlock growth, and how insight translates into execution across markets.  

Working closely with regional category, insights and innovation teams, you will turn deep consumer, shopper, customer and category insight into clear strategies, quantified growth opportunities and executable roadmaps.  

This role is permanent full time and can be based in Sydney or Melbourne.  

What You’ll Do  

Identify and lead category growth and disruption opportunities, including big‑bet innovations

Define and quantify category and portfolio‑led growth opportunities using integrated insight

Embed global category strategy into innovation pipelines, portfolio planning and market execution

Lead global portfolio category strategies and customer sell stories

Deliver global category performance reporting and competitor intelligence

Act as a trusted category expert to regional commercial and marketing teams

Build global category capability and share best practice across regions

About You  

8+ years’ experience in category strategy or category management  

Background in wine, alcohol or FMCG  

Proven track record delivering innovation‑led or portfolio‑led growth  

Strong strategic, analytical and commercial capability  

Comfortable influencing in complex, matrixed global organisations  

Experience integrating multiple data sources and turning insight into action  

Interest or experience using AI and advanced analytics   

Why Vinarchy  

Global scale with real strategic influence

High visibility and impact at the centre of growth decisions

Hybrid working (minimum three days in office)

Opportunity to help define the future of a global category

Staff wine allowance and access to unique wine specials

Paid Parental Leave

Access to an extensive online learning library

Employee Assistance Program

Exclusive employee discounts with national retailers

Keen to Apply?    

At Vinarchy, we are dedicated to building a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer.    

Prospective candidates will be required to provide proof of legal work rights and may be required to undertake relevant, role-appropriate pre‑employment screening.    

If you require any reasonable adjustments to participate in the recruitment process at any stage, please let us know.

Optus is a leading Australian telecommunications provider, delivering more than 11 million services every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

We're seeking a Risk Governance and Performance Manager to strengthen risk, controls, and compliance across our Consumer Business Unit. Working at the intersection of data, risk, and decision‑making, you'll translate complex information into clear insights that support leaders, protect customers, and drive effective frontline execution.

With end‑to‑end oversight of Consumer risk and compliance, you'll partner with senior leaders, risk and compliance teams, and analytics to identify trends, surface emerging risks, and support governance forums. This is an ideal role for someone who enjoys working with data, influencing stakeholders, and connecting risk frameworks to real customer, regulatory, and business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Create a consistent approach to governance within the Line 1 Risk & Controls team and play a lead role in preparing materials for Consumer Business unit governance forums and underlying business profile risk forums
Put that approach into practice, collaborating with other members of the line 1 risk team and business partners to assemble governance materials that provide timely and accurate information to enable oversight and decision making.
Design and deliver automated reporting tools and dashboard that provides clear visibility of controls performance, issues, incidents, risks and compliance obligations, enabling day to day management of risk as well as good governance outcomes across Consumer.
Establish and track key risk indicators (KRIs), control metrics, and leading indicators aligned to risk appetite.
Analyse data to identify control gaps, systemic issues, and emerging risks, escalating material findings through governance forums.
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights to support senior leadership decision‑making.
Partner with Line 1, Line 2, analytics, IT, and risk teams to support assurance activities, control uplift initiatives, and reporting automation.
Act as a trusted advisor to Consumer leaders on control effectiveness and risk performance, while championing a strong risk‑based culture.

What makes you perfect

5+ years' experience in governance, audit, controls performance, risk, compliance, assurance, quality, or insights‑led roles—ideally in a regulated, customer‑facing environment.
Demonstrated experience preparing written materials for senior governance forums related to risk and compliance matters
Strong understanding of risk and control frameworks, governance, and regulatory obligations.
Demonstrated experience delivering meaningful reporting, analytics, or assurance outcomes for senior stakeholders.
Strong analytical capability, with the ability to turn data into practical, outcome‑focused insights.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence influencing across multiple functions, and experience in coaching others to improve outcomes.
A pragmatic, customer‑centric approach to risk—focused on improving outcomes, not checklist compliance.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity.

Company Description

Let’s create a more sociable future together

At Endeavour, we’re totally into what we do. With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, we love to bring people together. Job Description
Be uniquely you, come as you are 
Execute pricing vision & strategic agenda within BWS
An exciting opportunity to combine your strong analytical, pricing & strategic skills

As our Pricing Strategy Manager for BWS, you'll execute the strategic direction and governance of retail pricing across the brand.

It's about getting that balance right: keeping us profitable, staying competitive, and driving growth. You'll put our pricing frameworks into action, backed up by solid market smarts and data.

Work closely with other teams to make sure our pricing plays nicely with BWS's bigger commercial goals.

Sound good? Read on.

Here is a taster of what you can expect in this role:

Drive BWS pricing strategy, aligning with commercial, customer, and brand goals to boost profitability and competitiveness.
Partner with stakeholders to embed pricing as a core commercial lever, balancing value with customer experience.
Lead pricing innovation through a test-and-learn agenda, generating insights for continuous optimisation.
Collaborate on promotional pricing frameworks to maximise ROI and relevance.
Utilise analytics for BWS-specific insights, identifying inconsistencies, margin opportunities, and performance gaps.
Support pricing mechanics and customer offers by partnering with Loyalty, Digital, and Ecommerce teams.
Monitor BWS pricing performance against financial targets and customer strategies.
Improve operational efficiency through process innovation and capability uplift in pricing.
Act as the BWS pricing representative, building strategic partnerships to embed pricing into decision-making and gain alignment in a matrix environment.
Role model Endeavour Group’s Leadership Values and support the broader Customer Value agenda.
Qualifications

Now let’s talk about you: 

5+ years of experience in revenue management or pricing strategy, with a proven track record of success.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or similar.
Proven strategy implementation experience.
Strong analytical skills, including proficiency in financial modeling and data analysis tools.
Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey pricing strategies to diverse stakeholders.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.
Experience with market research and competitive analysis.
Retail experience is highly regarded.

The benefits are good too! 

We offer hybrid ways of working
An exclusive discount card for BWS, Dan Murphy’s, Woolworths, BIG W and other Endeavour Group brands, including our ALH pubs
Your health and wellbeing is your most important asset, and as one of our valued team members, it’s our first priority. You will have a range of free services to help you live well and support your physical, mental and financial wellbeing
Endeavour Group is full of opportunities - use our dedicated learning and development options to grow an idea, yourself, and your career. This is just the start, so dream big.

At Endeavour, we value being a workplace where everyone’s welcome - if you meet a number of the requirements (and not all), we encourage you to apply.

Additional Information

We are together creators

With a portfolio that includes Dan Murphy’s, BWS, ALH Hotels, Pinnacle Drinks and more, Endeavour Group is big on sociability. Together we create the moments that bring millions of people together. And together we have more fun, create more opportunities, and score a lot more goals. We’re serious about creating a safe, inclusive and fun place to rock up to where equal opportunity is key, and flexibility is part of how we roll.  

We’re all about creating a more sociable future - for our customers and each other. If this job excites you - and you’re close-enough on the requirements, reach out, we’d love to hear from you.

You can learn more about working with us on LinkedIn or at endeavourgroupcareers.com.au.

Our Talent Team and Hiring Leaders kindly request no unsolicited resumes or approaches from Recruitment Agencies. Endeavour Group is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

#WeAreTogetherCreators #ComeAsYouAre #DreamBig #FeelTheEnergy #LeaveYourMark #EndeavourGroup

Change Analyst - Contract

Job Title: Change Analyst
Location: Adelaide (2 days per week onsite)
Contract Length: 12 months initially
Day Rate: $600 per day inc super

About the Role

An exciting opportunity exists for an experienced Change Analyst to join a large-scale enterprise transformation program. This role will be instrumental in shaping and delivering change outcomes across complex business, process, and technology initiatives.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders, SMEs, and delivery teams to assess impacts, drive readiness, coordinate change activity across multiple streams, and ensure successful adoption of new ways of working. This is a hands-on role suited to someone who can operate autonomously, influence stakeholders, and lead change in a fast-paced program environment.

Requirements

Lead end-to-end change impact assessments across business, process, and technology domains
Develop and execute change strategies aligned to broader program objectives
Facilitate workshops with stakeholders, SMEs, and delivery teams
Build trusted relationships across business units, project teams, and leadership groups
Coordinate change activity across multiple workstreams to ensure consistency of messaging and delivery
Deliver business readiness assessments and implementation plans
Produce high-quality change artefacts including communications, training materials, stakeholder plans, and impact assessments
Support training design, capability uplift, transition to BAU, and go-live readiness
Track adoption outcomes and support post-implementation reviews
Identify and manage change risks, issues, and dependencies

Criteria

2+ years experience as a Change Analyst or similar role within large transformation programs
Proven ability to independently deliver impact assessments, readiness activities, and stakeholder engagement strategies
Experience working across complex business or technology change environments
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence senior stakeholders
Strong planning, analytical, and problem-solving capability
Experience translating project or technical change into clear business impacts and actions
Ability to manage competing priorities across multiple workstreams
Change qualifications such as Prosci highly regarded

Why Apply

This is an opportunity to join a high-profile transformation program delivering meaningful enterprise-wide change. You'll work across strategic initiatives, engage senior stakeholders, and play a key role in driving successful outcomes across the business. Ideal for a senior change professional seeking a challenging and visible role with strong long-term impact.

If this role is of interest, please apply or email your CV to karim.romdhane@precisionsourcing.com.au and we'll be in touch if your experience matches the role!

The Opportunity 
We are partnering exclusively with Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) who are embarking on a transformative 5-year investment plan. To ensure their strategic objectives translate into tangible outcomes, we are seeking a Benefits Realisation Lead to join the Stategy & Tranformation division under the enterprise PMO (ePMO).

This isn't a role for a spectator. You will be leading the Benefits Realisation Framework, bridging the gap between high-level strategy and operational data. You will work colloborate with Sponsors and Project Owners to identify, measure, and most importantly validate the value Brisbane Catholic Education deliver to their schools and communities.

Why this role?

Enterprise Impact: Lead the development and maintenance of the BCE Benefits Realisation Framework from the ground up.
Strategic ROI: Monitor and report on value realisation across a large  5-year enterprise portfolio.
Collaboration & Creativity: Facilitate workshops to drive engagement and shared responsibility for change and benefits.
Data-Driven: Leverage a wealth of data and Power BI to interpret dashboards and provide independent assurance of project success.
What you’ll bring:

The Framework Expertise: Proven experience in Benefits Realisation, Value Management, or a senior ePMO role where you have developed methodologies and tools.
Influence & Mentorship: You know how to coach & influence Sponsors and Project Managers, acting as the SME on best practices for ROI and business case alignment.
Analytical Rigor: A strong ability to interpret data (Power BI experience is highly regarded) to show ROI and value metrics.
Change Savvy: A background in either, Project Management, Change Management or Economics, with an understanding that benefits realisation is a shared journey.
Highly Desirable: Any experience or knowledge of the education sector.
The Benefit;
BCE offer more than just a competitive salary. You will benefit from 12.75% Employer Superannuation and Salary Packaging options that significantly increase your take-home pay. This is a chance to use your corporate strategy skills to make a genuine difference in the education of young Queenslanders.

Apply Now Please submit your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience and how it aligns to this position.

The Head of Digital & AI is a newly created, high-impact leadership role sitting at the intersection of internal transformation and client-facing advisory. You will be the driving force behind how we harness AI to operate smarter, deliver more value to clients, and build new capability - both inside our business and across the clients we work with.

This is a hands-on individual contributor role from day one, with a clear mandate to grow a practice and team beneath you as the function matures.

AI Enabled Operations (Internal)

Own and drive the AI Enabled Operations program — the end-to-end rollout of AI tooling across internal business functions.
Define the roadmap for AI adoption, prioritising initiatives by business value, feasibility, and strategic fit.
Measure and report on adoption metrics, efficiency gains, and ROI across deployed AI initiatives.
Champion a culture of AI-first thinking across operations, service delivery, and support functions.
Work cross-functionally with department heads to identify, pilot, and scale AI use cases.
Govern the responsible and secure use of AI tools in line with company policy and risk appetite.
Client AI Strategy & Advisory

Lead AI strategy consulting engagements with clients, acting as a trusted executive advisor on their AI journey.
Conduct executive-level workshops and briefings to help clients identify where AI can create meaningful business value.
Develop repeatable frameworks, assessments, and playbooks for client AI strategy engagements.
Collaborate with sales and account management teams to identify and progress AI advisory opportunities.
Represent the company as a thought leader in AI — contributing to proposals, events, and market positioning.
Strategy, Thought Leadership & Team Building

Develop and maintain the company's overarching Digital Experience & AI Strategy, keeping it current with market trends and emerging capability.
Build and articulate a compelling vision for how digital and AI capability differentiates our MSP offering.
Mentor and develop talent across the business with an interest in digital and AI disciplines
Experience

10+ years in technology strategy, consulting, or digital transformation roles.
Proven experience delivering AI or automation programs in a business operations context.
Hands-on experience in or leading software / application development — either in-house or in a consulting/agency setting.
Experience engaging C-suite and executive stakeholders, both internally and as clients.
Background in managed services, IT consulting, or professional services highly regarded.
Skills & Capabilities

Strong commercial acumen: able to build business cases, size opportunities, and articulate value.
Exceptional communicator: equally effective in a workshop room, boardroom, or written brief.
Ability to translate complex AI and technology concepts into clear, business-relevant language.
Pragmatic and delivery-focused: comfortable operating without a team or established playbook.
Entrepreneurial mindset: energised by building something new from the ground up.
Knowledge

Deep familiarity with the current AI landscape: LLMs, agentic AI, automation platforms, AI-assisted development tools.
Understanding of MSP business models, service delivery, and the IT landscape of mid-market organisations.
Familiarity with modern software development practices (Agile, DevOps, cloud-native).

About Ubank

At Ubank, we help the digital generation get ahead with money. We’re an everyday money companion, bringing savings, spending, and insights together in one smart, simple experience. From building better savings habits to buying your first home sooner, we’re here to help you move forward with confidence.

The Opportunity This role is for a data professional who loves turning complexity into clarity. You’ll help power data‑driven decisions across Ubank by building reliable pipelines, strong data models, and scalable analytics solutions.

You’re curious, technically hands‑on, and excited by modern platforms and tools. Most importantly, you want to shape how data is used at a fast‑growing, digital‑first bank.

What You’ll Do

Design and build end‑to‑end data pipelines that connect systems and enable trusted insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand the why, not just the what, behind data needs.
Model and map data flows aligned to our Data Vault architecture.
Embed strong documentation, testing, and quality controls across data assets.
Develop curated datasets and analytics solutions with Analysts and business teams.
Explore and adopt new data sources, platforms, and engineering techniques.
Collaborate with engineers, platform teams, and external partners to deliver at scale.
What You Bring
5+ years’ experience designing, building, and deploying complex data pipelines and datasets.
Strong hands‑on experience with data modelling, transformation, and preparation.
Experience with data streaming technologies (e.g. Kafka, Flink).
Solid knowledge of modern data platforms: data lakes, lakehouse/cloud environments, APIs, and relational databases.
Practical experience with CI/CD and Infrastructure‑as‑Code.
Strong SQL and proficiency in Python, Spark/PySpark, Java, and dbt.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills and a collaborative mindset.
A natural curiosity and ability to problem‑solve independently.
Nice to Have
Experience with Databricks.
Exposure to Growth, Marketing or Martech data domains.
Knowledge of 3NF data modelling.
Experience with AWS, Airflow, Fivetran, or cloud‑based PostgreSQL.
Why Ubank
Make real impact: Your work will directly influence how ubank grows and serves customers.
Innovate together: A highly collaborative culture where ideas are encouraged and supported.
Build something meaningful: We give you trust, tools, and freedom to create change.
Be yourself: Flexible hybrid working and a culture that values individuality.
Accelerate your career: Clear development paths and opportunities to grow fast.
This is a Sydney‑based permanent role and requires Australian or New Zealand citizenship or permanent residency.

We proudly acknowledge the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we work and live.

We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and can’t wait to connect.

Apply now with your CV and help shape the future of data at Ubank.

Please note, we’re not accepting unsolicited candidate submissions from recruitment agencies. We work with a dedicated panel of partners, so kindly ask agencies not to contact Ubank team members directly.

Job Title: Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst - Fund Accounting
Ad Text:
About the Company
Davidson is partnering with a well-regarded organisation within the superannuation sector to appoint an experienced Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst on a 12-month fixed term contract. This is an exciting opportunity to join a business delivering important change across investment operations, finance and technology, with a strong focus on fund accounting and unit pricing environments.

About the Role
The Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst will play a pivotal role in leading and shaping complex initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and broader investment operations. Sitting at the intersection of business, finance and technology, this role will be responsible for driving delivery outcomes while also leading business analysis activities to ensure requirements are clearly defined, agreed and translated into effective solutions.

This role will suit someone who can move comfortably between strategic stakeholder engagement, detailed business analysis and end-to-end project delivery.

Key responsibilities will include:

Leading end-to-end delivery of initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and investment operations
Partnering with business, finance and technology stakeholders to identify needs, define requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose solutions
Leading business analysis activities including requirement gathering, process mapping, impact assessments and documentation
Managing project plans, governance, risks, issues, dependencies and reporting across multiple workstreams

Driving workshops and stakeholder discussions to build alignment, clarify priorities and support decision-making
Supporting system and process improvements across investment and funds management operations
Coordinating internal teams and external vendors to ensure successful delivery and implementation outcomes
Acting as the key link between business stakeholders and delivery teams to ensure solutions align with operational and strategic objectives

About You
The ideal candidate will bring a blend of project management and lead business analysis capability, with a strong track record of delivering complex change within superannuation, investment operations or broader financial services environments.

You will bring:

Proven experience in a Project Manager, Lead Business Analyst or hybrid delivery role within financial services
Strong experience working across fund accounting and unit pricing environments
Deep understanding of superannuation, investments or funds management operations
Demonstrated ability to lead both project delivery and business analysis activities across finance and technology functions
Strong capability in requirements gathering, process analysis, workshop facilitation and stakeholder engagement
Experience working on system-led change initiatives in partnership with technology teams
Strong governance, planning, risk and issue management capability

The Company
Our client is a large ASX‑listed organisation undergoing a significant period of growth, portfolio optimisation and strategic asset activity. With a strong pipeline of acquisitions, divestments and major capital projects, they are seeking a commercially minded Finance Analyst to join their high‑performing corporate finance team. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced, deal‑driven environment and brings experience from projects, engineering, construction, infrastructure or other asset‑intensive industries.

The Role
Reporting to the CFO and working close with the Corporate Finance Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the organisation’s M&A activity, asset transactions and strategic investment decisions. Your work will directly influence executive‑level decision making and long‑term portfolio strategy. Some of your duties will include:

Financial analysis and modelling for acquisitions, divestments and capital projects
Supporting due diligence across financial, operational and commercial workstreams
Preparing valuation models, scenario analysis and investment papers
Supporting the month‑end and reporting process for M&A‑related accounting impacts
About You
You will be an experienced Finance Analyst who has worked in due diligence and M&A environments who is able to independently work on analysis of financials and also create scenario based models. Ideally you would have started your career in Big 4 or in a M&A consulting environment.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer / Data Scientist (Azure, SQL, LLMs)
Ad Text:
Why this role stands out

This isn’t a pure data science role sitting in notebooks.

It’s a hands-on, end-to-end position where you’ll design data pipelines, optimise large-scale SQL environments, and build real-world AI features used in production.

You’ll be working on a modern Azure platform, solving complex data problems, and helping shape how advanced analytics and LLM capabilities are embedded into core products.

If you enjoy building, not just modelling, this is worth a look.

What you’ll be doing
Designing and optimising ETL pipelines in Microsoft SQL Server
Implementing incremental data processing (CDC / Change Tracking)
Building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines in Azure DevOps
Developing and deploying data science and LLM-driven features
Working across Azure services to deliver scalable, production-grade solutions
Tuning and troubleshooting complex SQL workloads
Partnering with product and engineering teams to deliver data-driven functionality
What we’re looking for
Strong experience with Microsoft SQL Server (ETL, performance tuning, CDC)
Solid background (3 plus years) across data engineering and/or data science
Experience working in Azure environments
Exposure to CI/CD (Azure DevOps preferred)
Hands-on experience with LLMs or modern AI tooling
Ability to build production-ready solutions, not just prototypes
Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset
Full working rights in Australia

Real products, real users, real impact

What’s on offer
Competitive salary + super
Brisbane CBD location

Work on modern Azure and AI-driven systems
Genuine influence over architecture and direction
If you’re someone who enjoys getting into the detail, solving hard data problems, and seeing your work in production - this is a strong opportunity.

Are you passionate about data strategy, architecture, and empowering businesses with data-driven insights? Do you enjoy designing scalable solutions and guiding organizations through complex data challenges? At InterWorks, we’re looking for a Data Architect who thrives in dynamic environments and is ready to help shape the future of data for our clients.

This role blends the rigor of enterprise data architecture and governance with the energy of consulting and solution-building. You’ll work with clients across industries to build robust data platforms, define standards, and implement governance frameworks that create lasting value.

Please be advised that this role is required to be located in the Melbourne, Australia InterWorks office. Remote work or telecommuting arrangements outside of this jurisdiction are not permissible for this position.

Salary range commensurate with experience and qualifications.

What You’ll Do

Architect and implement enterprise-level data solutions that align with business goals and compliance requirements.

Design and optimize data models, storage solutions, and integration pipelines across various platforms (Snowflake, Databricks, etc.).

Partner directly with clients to develop scalable data platforms, data governance frameworks, and analytics strategies.

Create and oversee ETL/ELT processes and advise on best practices for data quality, security, and accessibility.

Serve as a strategic liaison between business and IT to bridge gaps and ensure alignment.

Manage project timelines, expectations, and deliverables with clear communication and strong stakeholder engagement.

What You’ll Need

Must-Haves

5+ years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related field

High proficiency in SQL and strong data modeling skills

Experience in data governance, compliance standards, and enterprise data strategy

Excellent communication skills—written, verbal, and interpersonal

Ability to thrive in client-facing environments and lead cross-functional teams

Adaptability and problem-solving mindset in fast-paced and ambiguous situations

Proven experience with data lakehouse architecture including the storage and processing of semi-structured and unstructured file types

Proven support for data science initiatives

Understanding of pipeline and cost monitoring and optimisation

Comfortable leading business engagements to source requirements or refine data models

Experience with Azure & AWS CSP's preferable

What We’d Like You to Have

Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)

Familiarity with modern data warehouses and cloud platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks)

ETL tool experience (e.g., dbt, Matillion, Informatica, Fivetran)

Knowledge of AI/ML concepts and their role in data platforms

Degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field (preferred)

Ad Text:
Passionate about education?

Want to help improve outcomes for schools and students?

Reviews and School Improvement (RSI) provides system monitoring and assurance to support continuous improvement in Queensland state schools and education centres by providing leadership and oversight for targeted insights reviews. In addition, the RSI contributes significantly to distilling and sharing evidence-based insights and effective practices, to inform school improvement across the Queensland education system. Insights reviews (behaviour) will provide schools with practical guidance to strengthen their discipline systems and practices, enabling every school to deliver excellence through maintaining high expectations, a positive learning culture and quality teaching and learning.

The successful candidate will be employed at the classification listed on the attached role description under EOSD provisions.

About the Role

RSI are seeking Principals, Insights Reviews (behaviour) with particular knowledge and experience in leading effective and responsive school systems and practices which support student engagement for learning and providing effective support and interventions for student with diverse learning needs and complex behaviours.

As the Principal, Insights Reviews you will:

Lead insights reviews (behaviour) across Queensland State Schools to inform school and system improvement.
Play a key role in the delivery of the insights reviews as a team member.
Contribute to development of insights reviews methodology and processes.

Responsibilities include:

Deliver insights reviews, aligned to system priorities and initiatives, using evidence-based tools and methodologies.
Conduct insights reviews in line with agreed methodologies and processes.
As chair, lead teams, including the support and mentoring of review team members.
Capture high-quality evidence in reports that generate insights to inform school, region and system decision making.
Identify and share effective practice.
Collaboratively develop and implement approaches responsive to current and emerging system priorities.
Foster high quality collaboration, communication, cultural awareness and information sharing to enable an understanding of the complexities and challenges that exist in specific targeted areas.
Develop a rapport and engage with a range of external and internal stakeholders including Principals, school leaders, teachers, school staff, students, parents, regional staff and carers and community members to effectively and collaboratively enable an integrated approach to deliver the vision of the department.
Provide advice and expertise to improve school systems and processes that promote engagement and educational outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.
Understand and apply the legislation and policies that influence schooling especially in relation to students with diverse and/or complex learning needs and young people who are not actively engaged in a learning pathway.
Develop, articulate and promote a shared educational vision focused on providing quality learning outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.

The timing and location of school reviews can be a determining factor in when the employee is appointed.

Successful applicants will be expected to attend training prior to undertaking the role.

Applicants must be available to travel, including overnight absences.

Mandatory Requirements:

You must be a permanent employee of the department with a substantive teaching or classified officer role employed under Teaching in State Education Award - State 2016, have current full registration as a teacher and should be aware that temporary internal moves for the purposes of the department's payroll processes are referred to as internal secondments
You must be currently employed as a Principal or Acting Principal in a Queensland state school
Employees engaged temporarily as Education Officer - Special Duties have a requirement of 36¼ hours per week attendance and arrangements as outlined in clause 7.4 of the Department of Education State School Teachers Certified Agreement 2019

Interested? To Apply: 

Attach a brief resume (maximum 2 pages) including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history)
Attach a maximum two-page response demonstrating your experiences in leading a school improvement agenda, utilising strong interpersonal skills to be an effective leader, and your suitability for the role
Completion of the Principal, Insights Reviews questionnaire (Please note: once you have submitted your application documents and pressed next, you will be prompted to answer the required questionnaire)

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Your new opportunity
Queensland Treasury plays a central role in supporting the Government's infrastructure and investment priorities. We are seeking a Principal Commercial Analyst to join our Commercial Project Advisory team within the Infrastructure Projects group.

In this role, you will work on complex and high-profile commercial matters, supporting government engagement with major infrastructure projects and private sector partners. You will contribute to practical, well-considered advice that informs investment decisions and delivery approaches across government, helping ensure projects are affordable, viable and deliver long-term value for Queenslanders.

This is an opportunity to apply your commercial expertise in a trusted central agency environment, where your work will directly influence outcomes of state-wide significance.

In this role, you will:
• Support the delivery of high-quality commercial, financial and transaction advice to government agencies and Treasury, particularly in relation to investment decisions, business cases, procurement and delivery models.
• Contribute to the development and assessment of delivery strategies, including Public Private Partnerships and other commercial models, by analysing risks, market capability and commercial structures.
• Analyse financial and commercial documentation such as feasibility assessments, financial models, cost estimates and demand forecasts to support robust, evidence-based recommendations.
• Support commercial assurance activities by constructively challenging assumptions, methodologies and commercial positions to strengthen project proposals.
• Build and manage effective working relationships with stakeholders across government and industry to support market engagement, negotiations and coordinated advice.
• Assist in resolving complex issues by contributing to timely, practical and outcome-focused solutions, including leading or supporting responses when issues arise.
• Build strong networks across government and industry to support successful project outcomes.

What experience you will bring:
• Demonstrated commercial experience in infrastructure, investment or major project environments, with a strong focus on value for money, affordability and appropriate risk allocation.
• Strong commercial acumen within a government, regulatory or policy context.
• Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work effectively across government, central agencies and industry to support coordinated, outcome-focused advice.
• The ability to apply commercial judgement in a government setting, synthesising financial, economic and strategic inputs to support sound investment decisions.

Whilst there are no mandatory qualifications needed for this role, relevant qualifications will be highly regarded.

How to apply
To submit your application, click on the Apply link to provide:
• Your resume detailing relevant experience
• A cover letter of no more than two pages outlining why you are interested in this position and how your knowledge, skills and experience makes you a strong candidate for the role.

Queensland Treasury is committed to building an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the community we serve and treating all applicants equitably. This starts with recruitment, including making reasonable adjustments to support applicants at all stages of the process.

Applications to remain current for 12 months
